library.description: النواة الأساسية لـ SDL3: النوافذ، الإدخال، الأحداث، الصوت، والرسم الأساسي.

kind.description: استدعاءات تنفذ فعلاً، تغيّر حالة، أو تعيد نتيجة، أو تبني مورداً.

summary.purposeArabic: تستخدم هذه الدالة عندما تحتاج إلى جلب الخصائص associated السطح داخل التطبيق أو داخل مسار التنفيذ المرتبط بها.
summary.whyUseArabic: القيمة الحقيقية هنا هي الأثر التنفيذي الذي يحدثه الاستدعاء أو النتيجة التي يعيدها إلى التطبيق.
summary.carriedValueArabic:
القيمة العملية لهذا الاستدعاء تظهر في ناتجه ( SDL_PropertiesID ) Returns a
valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information. وفي التغيير الذي يحدثه على المورد أو الحالة المرتبطة به.
summary.sourceDescription: Get the properties associated with a surface.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.descriptionArabic: يمثل المعامل surface من النوع SDL_Surface * القيمة أو المورد الذي يعتمد عليه هذا الاستدعاء.

remarks: The following properties are understood by SDL:

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT :
for HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the value of 100%
diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic
Range headroom. This defaults to 203 for HDR10 surfaces and 1.0 for
floating point surfaces.

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT :
for HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the maximum dynamic
range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This
defaults to 0.0, which disables tone mapping.

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_TONEMAP_OPERATOR_STRING :
the tone mapping operator used when compressing from a surface with high
dynamic range to another with lower dynamic range. Currently this
supports "chrome", which uses the same tone mapping that Chrome uses for
HDR content, the form "*=N", where N is a floating point scale factor
applied in linear space, and "none", which disables tone mapping. This
defaults to "chrome".

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HOTSPOT_X_NUMBER :
the hotspot pixel offset from the left edge of the image, if this
surface is being used as a cursor.

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HOTSPOT_Y_NUMBER :
the hotspot pixel offset from the top edge of the image, if this surface
is being used as a cursor.

SDL_PROP_SURFACE_ROTATION_FLOAT :
the number of degrees a surface's data is meant to be rotated clockwise
to make the image right-side up. Default 0. This is used by the camera
API, if a mobile device is oriented differently than what its camera
provides (i.e. - the camera always provides portrait images but the
phone is being held in landscape orientation). Since SDL 3.4.0.
details.returns:
( SDL_PropertiesID ) Returns a
valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError () for more information.
details.threadSafety: It is safe to call this function from any thread.
